danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite collision with hostile entities (demon, T-Rex, ninja, pirate, mad scientist, vampire)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; triggers damage state or form-change

danger_signals.visual_form: Congo visually transforms from human to monkey form upon enemy hit
danger_signals.meaning: First damage taken; player enters vulnerable state with one hit remaining before life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Large unique sprite entities with distinct designs per world (demon, T-Rex, ninja, pirate, mad scientist, vampire)
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat and progression gate; world-state culmination

danger_signals.visual_form: World-specific obstacles across jungle, mountain, pirate ship, volcano, ghost town settings
danger_signals.meaning: Contextual danger tied to world-state thematic identity

safety_signals.visual_form: Congo rendered as half-human, half-monkey character sprite in default state
safety_signals.meaning: Full health/protection; capable of sustaining two hits before life loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Advancement through 35 levels across distinct world zones
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone transitions communicate world-state shifts and narrative progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Save/resume mechanic (noted as feature in reviews)
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency; ability to return to safe progression state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Half-human, half-monkey hybrid character; romantic quest narrative
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in all worlds; rescues girlfriend from demon abduction
Boss encounter; narrative linchpin for rescue quest

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: T-Rex (prehistoric danger), Ninja (oriental threat), Pirate (nautical lawlessness), Mad Scientist (chaotic disruption), Vampire (supernatural evil)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each boss type represents distinct world context
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Thematic alignment with respective worlds (volcano/T-Rex, pirate ship/Pirate, ghost town/Vampire, etc.)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tropical environment with flora, fauna, natural hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Early-game baseline; establishes rescue narrative context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High-altitude terrain, vertical level design, alpine aesthetics
Naval vessel setting with nautical obstacles, water hazards, ship architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounter with pirate faction; maritime-themed challenges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lava environments, volcanic hazards, geothermal aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: T-Rex boss arena; thematic alignment of prehistoric and volcanic imagery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Abandoned settlement, supernatural atmosphere, spectral aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vampire boss encounter; late-game supernatural escalation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Congo reverts to monkey form visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One hit remaining before life loss; heightened player tension state

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of 5 world zones (jungle, mountain, pirate ship, volcano, ghost town) establishes narrative context and visual identity through environment design and boss type alignment
Congo's transformation from human to monkey visually narrates damage progression and vulnerability without UI overlay
Each world's boss embodies that world's danger archetype (T-Rex = prehistoric, Pirate = lawlessness, Vampire = supernatural), making thematic coherence visible
Girlfriend abduction by demon establishes narrative drive across all 35 levels; visual progression through worlds communicates narrative advancement

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full combat capability; standard platforming and enemy engagement
One hit remaining; player behavior shifts to avoidance-focused gameplay
Large sprite presentation; unique visual design per boss type; world-state culmination markers
Context-specific obstacle communication (volcanic lava, nautical water, mountain cliffs, etc.)

notes: Congo's Caper uses minimal explicit UI for world-state communication; danger and safety are primarily conveyed through protagonist form state (human vs. monkey) and environmental visual design. The game eschews faction complexity, focusing instead on a straightforward rescue narrative with thematically aligned boss encounters across diverse world zones. Visual communication relies on sprite-based enemy identification and world aesthetics rather than symbolic markers. The lack of color-coding or faction insignia reflects the game's action-platformer paradigm prioritizing immediate spatial/visual hazard recognition over political or allegiance signaling.
